title: Bafana Bafana coach reveals the man he wants to replace him
description: FOOTBALL NEWS - Bafana Bafana head coach...
bodyText:
FOOTBALL NEWS - Bafana Bafana head coach Hugo Broos has thrown his support behind Helman Mkhalele taking over from him when he steps down after the 2026 Fifa World Cup finals.

The Citizen reports that Mkhalele has been an assistant coach to Broos since he took the job in 2021, and the two have formed an excellent working relationship that has gone a long way to restoring a nation’s pride in Bafana.


> ‘I think the best thing …’


South Africa finished third at the most recent Africa Cup of Nations finals in the Ivory Coast, will play in the competition again in Morocco at the end of this year and most recently celebrated qualification for next year’s world cup finals in the US, Canada and Mexico.

“I think the best thing … would be if Helman becomes coach after me, then you are following up with the same thing,” Broos told a media round table in Johannesburg yesterday.

“The way of working and the philosophy will stay the same. There will be a bit more of an African thing, I am European. But Helman thinks 95% the same as me, that is why I am so happy with him.”

Helman Mkhalele and Hugo Broos watch on during training. Photo: Blake Linder/Lowvelder

Broos did note, however, that Mkhalele does not currently have the required Uefa or Caf Pro Licence needed to be head coach of Bafana.

“The only problem is that Helman doesn’t have the qualification,” added Broos.

“You can avoid that if Helman goes on a course for the … pro licence. You (Safa) have to think about it, and it is not my job to do that, but you have to think about what kind of coach you want.”


> ‘It’s time to go home’


Broos remains committed to retiring from coaching and returning to Belgium after the world cup finals.

“Everyone knows I will stop after the world cup because of my age,” he said.

“It is not because I don’t want to be here anymore. If I was 10 years younger you can be sure I would have wanted to stay for another two years. I am very happy here. But I will be 74. I read I will be the oldest (Bafana) coach. I can’t be happy with that!

“It is time to go home to my family. They have made sacrifices over the last 30 years because they wanted me to focus on my career. Now it will be time to focus on them.”

Article: Caxton Network News
